seoTitle: How to Build a Report in Zendesk Explore Step by Step
metaDescription: Learn how to build a report in Zendesk Explore, from selecting a dataset and metrics to formatting visuals and compiling it into a dashboard.
intro: Guessing at team performance instead of measuring it can lead to missed problems in your support operation. This guide walks through building a custom report in Zendesk Explore from scratch, choosing the right dataset, applying metrics and attributes, and turning it into a chart you can share.

steps.heading: Open the Reports sidebar
steps.body: Navigate to the top product matrix menu, open Analytics, and select the Reports sidebar panel.

steps.heading: Select a source dataset
steps.body: Click New Report and choose your intended dataset, such as Support - Tickets.

steps.heading: Add a count metric
steps.body: Open the Metrics block and add a count variable like Tickets or Full Resolution Time.

steps.heading: Add categorization attributes
steps.body: Move to the Rows or Columns panel to add attributes like Assignee Name or Ticket Priority.

steps.heading: Constrain the date range
steps.body: Click the top Filters bar and scope the dataset to a safe date window, such as the past 30 days.

steps.heading: Choose a visualization and save
steps.body: Use the visualization tool palette to switch the layout into a chart, type a report title, and click Save.

faq.question: Why is applying a date filter important?
faq.answer: It prevents execution timeout errors that can occur when querying large, unscoped datasets.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple attributes in one report?
faq.answer: Yes, you can pair several attributes, like assignee and priority, to slice the same metric multiple ways.

faq.question: How do I share a finished report with my team?
faq.answer: Transfer your saved report into a multi-widget dashboard template for shared visibility.

faq.question: Does building reports slow down live tickets?
faq.answer: No, Explore's reporting engine queries background metrics without modifying active customer cases.
